session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
Read the following source files and report relevant sections:
1. `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/incremental_dispatch.py` - Look at lines 130-145 for retrack_branch() and index sync patterns 2. `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_helpers.py` - Look at the `ensure_trunk_synced` function, particularly the `find_worktree_for_branch` LBYL check and conditional git pull vs update_local_ref logic 3. `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/teleport_cmd.py` - Look at lines 195-235 for `_register_with_graphite()` and stacked vs non-stacked PR handling
Report the exact function names, line numbers, and key logic patterns.

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)

1→"""Helper functions for dispatch command.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 6→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext 7→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 8→ 9→ 10→def _check_trunk_worktree_clean(ctx: ErkContext, repo: RepoContext, *, trunk: str) -> None: 11→ """Raise SystemExit(1) if trunk is checked out in a dirty worktree.""" 12→ trunk_worktree = ctx.git.worktree.find_worktree_for_branch(repo.root, trunk) 13→ if trunk_worktree is None: 14→ return 15→ if not ctx.git.status.has_uncommitted_changes(trunk_worktree): 16→ return 17→ user_output( 18→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") 19→ + f"Worktree at {trunk_worktree} has {trunk} checked out with " 20→ f"uncommitted changes.\n\n" 21→ f"Please commit or stash changes before running erk pr dispatch." 22→ ) 23→ raise SystemExit(1) 24→ 25→ 26→def ensure_trunk_synced(ctx: ErkContext, repo: RepoContext) -> None: 27→ """Ensure local trunk ref is synced with remote, without requiring checkout. 28→ 29→ Uses fetch + update_local_ref to advance the local trunk branch pointer 30→ without needing trunk to be checked out. If trunk IS checked out in a
... [64 lines omitted] ...
33→ Raises SystemExit(1) on sync failure with clear error message. 45→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") 73→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") 82→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") 86→ f"Warning: This will discard any local commits on {trunk}."

1→"""Teleport a PR's remote state to the local branch. 2→ 3→Remote-first counterpart to 'checkout'. While checkout preserves local state 4→(reusing existing worktrees, keeping unpushed commits), teleport force-resets 5→the local branch to match the remote exactly. Use teleport when a remote agent 6→has pushed commits and you want your local copy to match. 7→ 8→Operates on the current worktree by default, with --new-slot to create a fresh 9→worktree slot. 10→""" 11→ 12→from pathlib import Path 13→ 14→import click 15→ 16→from erk.cli.activation import ( 17→ activation_config_activate_only, 18→ ensure_worktree_activate_script, 19→ print_activation_instructions, 20→) 21→from erk.cli.commands.checkout_helpers import ensure_branch_has_worktree 22→from erk.cli.commands.pr.checkout_cmd import _fetch_and_update_branch 23→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 24→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 25→from erk.core.repo_discovery import NoRepoSentinel, RepoContext 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRNotFound 27→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 28→ 29→ 30→@click.command("teleport")

session.tool_result:
1→"""Dispatch a local plan against an existing PR for remote implementation. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec incremental-dispatch --plan-file <path> --pr <number> [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→Commits the plan as impl-context to the PR's branch and triggers the 7→plan-implement workflow. Unlike regular dispatch, this does not require 8→the erk-plan label — just an OPEN PR. 9→""" 10→ 11→import json 12→from pathlib import Path 13→ 14→import click 15→ 16→from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_cmd import load_workflow_config 17→from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_helpers import ensure_trunk_synced 18→from erk.cli.commands.ref_resolution import resolve_dispatch_ref 19→from erk.cli.constants import DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME 20→from erk_shared.context.helpers import ( 21→ require_branch_manager, 22→ require_context, 23→ require_git, 24→ require_github, 25→ require_repo_root, 26→ require_time, 27→) 28→from erk_shared.context.types import NoRepoSentinel 29→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError 30→from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import (
... [189 lines omitted] ...
83→ _output_error(output_format, f"PR #{pr_number} not found") 87→ _output_error(output_format, f"PR #{pr_number} is {pr_result.state}, must be OPEN") 98→ _output_error(output_format, "Not in a git repository") 147→ if isinstance(push_result, PushError): 148→ _output_error(output_format, f"Push failed: {push_result.message}") 208→def _output_error(output_format: str, message: str) -> None: 209→ """Output error in the requested format.""" 211→ click.echo(json.dumps({"success": False, "error": message})) 213→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + message)
session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the source files. Let me compile the findings in a comprehensive report.
## FINDINGS REPORT
I have successfully located and analyzed the three source files you requested. Here are the key functions, line numbers, and logic patterns:
---
### 1. **incremental_dispatch.py** (Correct location: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/incremental_dispatch.py`)
**Function: `retrack_branch()` - Lines 133**
```python # Line 132-133: branch_manager = require_branch_manager(ctx) branch_manager.retrack_branch(repo_root, branch_name) ```
**Index Sync Patterns (Lines 102-111):**
```python # Line 102-111: Branch synchronization without checkout requirement # Sync local branch ref to remote (no checkout required) user_output(f"Syncing branch: {click.style(branch_name, fg='cyan')}") git.remote.fetch_branch(repo_root, "origin", branch_name) checked_out_path = git.worktree.is_branch_checked_out(repo_root, branch_name) if checked_out_path is None: git.branch.create_branch(repo_root, branch_name, f"origin/{branch_name}", force=True) else: remote_sha = git.branch.get_branch_head(repo_root, f"origin/{branch_name}") if remote_sha is not None: git.branch.update_local_ref(repo_root, branch_name, remote_sha) ```
**Key Pattern:** After committing files via `commit_files_to_branch()`, the code uses `retrack_branch()` to update Graphite tracking (line 133), then conditionally writes files to disk if the branch is currently checked out (lines 136-141).
---
### 2. **dispatch_helpers.py** (Location: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_helpers.py`)
**Function: `ensure_trunk_synced()` - Lines 26-88**
**LBYL Check Pattern (Lines 60-68):**
```python # Line 60-68: Conditional git pull vs update_local_ref logic trunk_worktree = ctx.git.worktree.find_worktree_for_branch(repo.root, trunk)
user_output(f"Syncing {trunk} with origin/{trunk}...") if trunk_worktree is not None: # Trunk is checked out — must use pull to update index + working tree ctx.git.remote.pull_branch(trunk_worktree, "origin", trunk, ff_only=True) else: # Trunk not checked out — safe to update ref directly ctx.git.branch.update_local_ref(repo.root, trunk, remote_sha) ```
**Key Logic:** - **Line 12-14:** LBYL check in `_check_trunk_worktree_clean()`: `if trunk_worktree is None: return` - **Lines 53-88:** Three-way merge analysis: - **Lines 56-69:** Local is behind remote → fast-forward (uses pull if checked out, else update_local_ref) - **Lines 70-78:** Local is ahead of remote → error (user has unpushed commits) - **Lines 79-88:** Diverged → error (both have unique commits)
**Critical Pattern:** When trunk is checked out in a worktree, use `pull_branch()` to update both ref AND working tree. When NOT checked out, use `update_local_ref()` for direct ref updates without checkout.
---
### 3. **teleport_cmd.py** (Location: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/teleport_cmd.py`)
**Function: `_register_with_graphite()` - Lines 198-232**
**Stacked vs Non-Stacked PR Handling (Lines 215-232):**
```python # Line 213: Detect trunk branch trunk = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
# Line 216-223: Stacked PR handling (base is not trunk) if base_ref_name != trunk: local_branches = ctx.git.branch.list_local_branches(repo.root) if base_ref_name not in local_branches: ctx.console.info(f"Fetching base branch '{base_ref_name}'...") ctx.git.remote.fetch_branch(repo.root, "origin", base_ref_name) ctx.branch_manager.create_tracking_branch( repo.root, base_ref_name, f"origin/{base_ref_name}" )
# Line 225-232: Graphite registration (applies to all PRs) parent = ctx.branch_manager.get_parent_branch(repo.root, branch_name) if parent is None: ctx.console.info("Tracking branch with Graphite...") ctx.branch_manager.track_branch(repo.root, branch_name, base_ref_name) else: if ctx.graphite_branch_ops is not None: ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(repo.root, branch_name) ```
**Key Patterns:** 1. **Lines 216-223:** For **stacked PRs** (base ≠ trunk), fetch the base branch locally and create a tracking branch 2. **Lines 226-232:** For **all PRs**: - If no parent exists: call `track_branch()` (initial registration) - If parent exists: call `retrack_branch()` via graphite_branch_ops (update existing tracking) 3. **LBYL Check (line 231):** `if ctx.graphite_branch_ops is not None:` before calling retrack
---
### Summary Table
| File | Function | Lines | Key Pattern | |------|----------|-------|------------| | incremental_dispatch.py | `retrack_branch()` call | 133 | Call after committing; update Graphite after plumbing changes | | incremental_dispatch.py | Index sync pattern | 102-111 | LBYL: fetch, check if checked out, then either create_branch or update_local_ref | | dispatch_helpers.py | `ensure_trunk_synced()` | 26-88 | LBYL: find_worktree_for_branch, then pull (if checked out) vs update_local_ref (if not) | | dispatch_helpers.py | Merge analysis | 53-88 | Three-way: behind (fast-forward), ahead (error), diverged (error) | | teleport_cmd.py | `_register_with_graphite()` | 198-232 | Stacked: fetch base if needed; all: track (new) or retrack (existing) with LBYL check |
